input_description: Clean single-sheet export — one pay period — correct headers — no calculated rows
learning_objective: Confirm the system works correctly with well-formed input before testing edge cases
actual_output: Tested with real personal Excel export — system correctly blocked and identified pay period header rows/invalid dates/calculated rows/missing income/multi-period sections
quality_rating: 5 — Real user CSV was tested instead of a clean baseline; system correctly caught all real-world formatting issues and blocked advice
notes: True well-formed baseline not yet tested — this run used the real Excel budget which has known formatting issues
patterns_observed: Real-world exports are rarely clean enough to pass validation without fixes
improvement_ideas: Create a minimal clean CSV to confirm the system gives good advice when data is well-formed

input_description: Export with pay period row (e.g. June 1–15) above column headers
expected_challenge: Parser reads pay period row as the header row, misidentifies columns
learning_objective: Determine whether the system detects and skips extra header rows or silently misreads the file
actual_output: Flagged pay period header and currency spacing issues but proceeded with advice anyway
quality_rating: 3 — Partial: flagged the issue but gave advice on bad data instead of blocking
notes: Should have blocked like it does for severely malformed files; flagged but didn't enforce
patterns_observed: Inconsistent validation threshold — minor issues get flagged but don't stop advice; severe issues do
improvement_ideas: Tighten validation rules so a misidentified header row always blocks advice

input_description: Export includes Total Income and Remaining rows mixed in with data rows
expected_challenge: Calculated rows parsed as income entries, inflating totals and distorting the budget picture
learning_objective: Determine whether the system ignores or correctly identifies non-data rows
actual_output: Blocked advice and told user to delete Total Income and Remaining rows; also caught leading-space issue in Amount column
quality_rating: 5 — Handled perfectly: blocked on bad data and gave clear fix instructions
patterns_observed: Correctly identified both the calculated rows and a secondary formatting issue

input_description: Full workbook export with multiple sheets (one per month)
expected_challenge: System reads only first sheet and ignores the rest, or fails entirely
learning_objective: Determine how the system handles multi-sheet exports and whether it prompts the user to export one sheet
actual_output: Blocked advice; caught repeating header rows from multiple pay periods and gave concrete fix instructions including a suggested Pay Period column structure
quality_rating: 5 — Handled perfectly: blocked on bad data and gave clear actionable fix
patterns_observed: Detected structural issue caused by stacked pay period sections with repeated headers

input_description: Some Amount or Due Date cells are blank (bills not yet due, variable income)
expected_challenge: Empty cells cause parsing errors or produce null values in advice
learning_objective: Determine whether the system handles empty cells gracefully without producing bad calculations
actual_output: Blocked advice; caught missing amounts for Car payment and Groceries and empty Side income row; also flagged missing due dates; gave numbered fix instructions
quality_rating: 5 — Handled perfectly: blocked on bad data and identified all empty cell issues clearly
patterns_observed: Caught multiple empty cell issues including a secondary missing due date issue

input_description: Amount column contains formatted values like $1,500.00 instead of raw numbers
expected_challenge: Currency symbols and commas cause Amount to be read as text, not numbers
learning_objective: Determine whether the system strips formatting and parses correctly or flags the issue to the user
actual_output: Inconsistent across runs — curl run flagged issue but gave advice anyway; browser run blocked and refused to advise
quality_rating: 3 — Inconsistent: one run gave advice on bad data, one correctly blocked
notes: Non-deterministic behavior — same CSV produced different outcomes on different runs
patterns_observed: LLM non-determinism means validation behavior is not reliably consistent across calls
improvement_ideas: Add deterministic pre-processing layer to strip/flag currency formatting before sending to Claude

input_description: Blank rows between Income and Bills sections used as visual separators
expected_challenge: Blank rows split the dataset into fragments and may break parsing
learning_objective: Determine whether the system handles blank separator rows without corrupting the data read
actual_output: Blocked advice; caught blank separator rows and flagged shifted Amount column and merged section structure; gave numbered fix steps
quality_rating: 5 — Handled perfectly: blocked on bad data and identified all structural issues clearly
patterns_observed: Detected blank rows and downstream column shifting they caused

input_description: Export without the Paid column — user exports a subset of columns
expected_challenge: System looks for Paid column, fails or silently skips payment status in advice
learning_objective: Determine whether the system flags the missing column and tells the user what's needed
actual_output: Flagged column misalignment in income rows but never mentioned the missing Paid column — missed the actual test scenario
quality_rating: 2 — Poor: gave advice but identified the wrong issue; missing Paid column went undetected
notes: Missed the key issue (missing Paid column) and flagged an unrelated structural concern instead
patterns_observed: System does not reliably detect missing expected columns; gets distracted by other formatting signals
improvement_ideas: Add explicit check for required columns (Income/Bills/Due Date/Amount/Paid) before any other validation
